fragility_points: Round 13 could sharply compress the lead.
A tie-break path could emerge if Sindarov stumbles.
An official dispute or result correction would matter more than generic strength context.
key_assumptions: The contextual standings showing Sindarov at 9/12 after 12 rounds are materially accurate.
No procedural or fair-play issue materially alters official results.
A two-point lead with two rounds left usually converts into an official win in this format.
main_logical_chain: Start from the 95.05% market price as a serious prior.
Ask what would justify that price: an overwhelming late-stage live lead rather than generic pre-event dominance.
Contextual standings indicate exactly that state, with Sindarov on 9/12 and nearest chaser on 7/12 after 12 rounds.
Because FIDE is the resolution authority and two rounds remain, trim modestly for residual conversion and verification risk.
Land at 91%, broadly agreeing with the market but slightly below it.
main_thesis: The market is directionally right because Sindarov appears to have an almost decisive late-stage lead, but 95.05% is slightly too high given residual two-round, tiebreak, and official-live-verification risk.
quote_anchors: official information from FIDE is the primary resolution source
two rounds remain before any tie-break day
source_quality_view: Resolution authority is high-quality and clear via FIDE, while the live-state evidence is strong but more contextual than ideal, so overall source quality is medium-high rather than fully high.
strongest_disconfirmers: Two rounds plus possible tie-breaks still leave a nonzero failure path.
Official FIDE live standings were not cleanly extracted in this run, so some confidence relies on contextual sources.
strongest_supports: Contextual live standings show Sindarov leading by two points with two rounds left.
The tournament is only 14 rounds, so there are few remaining opportunities for the field to catch up.
Sindarov is already an elite player, making conversion more credible than for a fluke leader.
timing_relevance: Very high; this is a late-stage in-progress tournament market where one round can change the entire tail-risk profile.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact official live standings were not directly captured from a clean FIDE page in this run.
Residual tie scenarios depend on remaining round outcomes not yet played.
what_would_change_view: A clean official FIDE standings/result update showing a smaller lead, a round-13 stumble, or any procedural dispute would push the estimate down materially.
